title: Dog Sneezing Pink Frothy Liquid: An Emergency Guide
meta_desc: Pink frothy liquid from a dog's nose is a critical emergency. Learn about the causes of pulmonary edema and why immediate vet care is required.
content_html:
What Is Pink Frothy Discharge?

If your dog is producing pink, foamy, or frothy liquid from their nose or mouth, it is often a sign of pulmonary edema. This occurs when fluid leaks into the air sacs of the lungs. The frothiness is caused by the fluid mixing with air as your dog breathes, and the pink color indicates that a small amount of blood has mixed in with that fluid.

Urgency Level: High (Emergency)

This is a life-threatening medical emergency. When froth appears at the nose, it means your dog is struggling to breathe and is not getting enough oxygen. This can be caused by congestive heart failure, trauma, toxin exposure, or near-drowning. You should stop reading and transport your dog to the nearest 24-hour emergency veterinary hospital immediately.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

If you can safely take a quick photo of the discharge or a video of your dog's breathing without delaying your trip to the vet, please do so. Seeing the consistency and color of the fluid helps the veterinary triage team immediately determine the severity of the lung congestion and allows them to prepare oxygen therapy and life-saving medications before you even walk through the door.

faqs.question: Can heart disease cause pink froth?
faqs.answer: Yes, left-sided congestive heart failure is one of the most common reasons for fluid buildup in the lungs (pulmonary edema) leading to pink frothy discharge.

faqs.question: What other symptoms might I see?
faqs.answer: You may notice your dog gasping for air, having blue or purple-tinged gums, or standing with their elbows pointed outward to try and expand their chest.

faqs.question: Should I try to clear the fluid myself?
faqs.answer: No. Do not attempt to clear the nose or mouth with instruments or fingers, as this can cause additional stress and worsen the respiratory distress. Focus entirely on rapid transport to a clinic.
